text.title: framework-decomposition-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for framework-decomposition-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Creating UserService.get_user_by_id() that just wraps User.objects.get().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wrappers without behavior are noise. They add indirection without adding testability or clarity. Use selectors only when behavior matters (filtering, joining, prefetching).

failure-mode.description: Rails service objects for simple CRUD that ActiveRecord handles natively.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Adds ceremony without benefit. Use Rails idioms for standard CRUD; service objects for multi-step actions with side effects (email, payment, async jobs).

failure-mode.description: React custom hook used in exactly one component and under 30 LOC.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inline the logic — a hook used once adds indirection without discoverability benefit. Keep related logic colocated until you have a reuse signal.
